title: Power Query folder ingestion with temp-file filtering, dynamic expansion, and stable order
originSignal: Folder ingestion query reads workbooks from a folder and must preserve row order, join cardinality, and dynamic columns.
riskPattern: Removing Folder.Files filters, replacing Excel.Workbook behavior, hard-coding expansion columns, or grouping without restoring order can change output rows.
regressionChecks: Temp workbook files are filtered before Excel.Workbook parsing.
Dynamic expansion derives columns from shaped nested tables instead of hard-coding a fixed list.
Grouping preserves first-seen business rows and restores explicit sort order after joins.
Lookup joins are duplicate-safe and cannot multiply main-table row count.
expectedEvidence: Power Query lineage/source-risk report identifies folder, local path, dynamic expansion, joins, and any native query risk.
Power Query M lint report warns when risky Folder.Files, join, or expansion patterns appear.
Task-specific validation records row counts before and after grouping/joining.
boundaries: Static M inspection cannot prove refresh success.
Live refresh evidence requires Windows desktop Excel and the source files copied to a safe task-local folder.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Add a sanitized workbook plus small source folder that reproduces folder ingestion, temp-file filtering, dynamic expansion, and row-count preservation.
